title.si: කෙසෙල් වගාව — තෙත් හා අතරමැදි කලාප

summary.en: FRDI recommendation for banana (Musa spp.). Successful cultivation from sea level to 1500 m in zones receiving 1250–1900 mm rainfall at 27–30 °C. Dessert varieties for wet + intermediate zones include Ambul (AAA), Kolikuttu (AAB), Anamalu (AAA), Cavendish (AAA). Cooking types include Alu Kesel and Mondan (ABB). Traditional 3 × 3 m or 3.5 × 3.5 m spacing; dense 3 × 1 m for commercial blocks. Per-plant fertilizer program. First bunch 12 weeks after flowering.
summary.si: කෙසෙල් (Musa spp.) සඳහා FRDI නිර්දේශය. 27-30 °C උෂ්ණත්වයේ සහ වාර්ෂික වර්ෂාපතනය 1250-1900 mm ලැබෙන ප්‍රදේශවල මුහුදු මට්ටමේ සිට 1500 m දක්වා සාර්ථකව වගා කළ හැකිය. තෙත් හා අතරමැදි කලාප සඳහා තේරීමේ ප්‍රභේද: ඇඹුල් (AAA), කොළිකුට්ටු (AAB), අනමාලු (AAA), කැවන්ඩිෂ් (AAA). උයා කන ප්‍රභේද: අලු කෙසෙල් සහ මොන්දන් (ABB). සම්ප්‍රදායික පරතරය 3 × 3 m හෝ 3.5 × 3.5 m; වාණිජ වශයෙන් 3 × 1 m. පැළයකට වූ පොහොර වැඩසටහන. පිපීමෙන් සති 12 කින් පළමු පොකුර.

field_provenance.notes: FRDI English page is partially under construction; this fixture captures the cultivation-critical fields published as of April 2026. Yield is not published in standard t/ha form on the source, so the yield range fields are omitted — bunch-weight-based yield lands in a follow-up pass.

body.en: Select sites with 1250–1900 mm rainfall at 27–30 °C; sea level to 1500 m. Dig 60 × 60 × 60 cm pits at 3 × 3 m or 3.5 × 3.5 m (traditional) or 3 × 1 m (dense commercial).
Mix basal fertilizer into each pit 2 weeks before planting the sucker. In wet zone add dolomite 500 g per pit for pH adjustment; add Kieserite where Mg is deficient.

notes.en: Applied per pit, not per area

body.en: Plant sword suckers 70–80 cm tall at the prepared pit. Sword > maiden > water shoots for vigour. Tissue-culture plants are also acceptable.
Harvest 12 weeks after flowering. Anamalu/Ambul 3 months; Kolikuttu 4 months; Bing Cavendish at 1.5–1.7 m pseudostem height. Cut the whole bunch at the peduncle; avoid bruising.

notes.en: Panama disease (Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. cubense). Soil-borne; chemical control ineffective. Management: improve drainage, plant resistant varieties (Ambul is the only recommended-resistant variety in Sri Lanka), use clean propagation material, avoid replanting on infected land for 5+ years.

Bunchy top virus — transmitted by the banana aphid (Pentalonia nigronervosa). No chemical cure. Rogue and destroy infected plants immediately; control aphid vectors; source tissue-culture or certified clean suckers.
Yellow Sigatoka (Mycosphaerella musicola). Spray chlorothalonil or mancozeb at 2-week intervals on susceptible black-bunch cultivars.
Anthracnose is severe on Alu Kesel. Spray mancozeb or chlorothalonil at 2-week intervals; bag the bunches after flowering to reduce infection.
